system.description:
Days 5–10 of the journey: from the Greypass toll through the mountain road, over the Titan's Stair, and down to Stairwatch. The terrain shifts from political obstacles to environmental ones — altitude, rockfalls, gorge crossings, blizzards, and the sheer grinding endurance of the Stair itself.

By the time the party crests the Stair, they should feel every one of the two hundred miles behind them.

complications.round1:
Round 1 — The Mountain Road (Days 5–8)

The party leaves the Greypass toll behind and climbs into the Midgard's Shield foothills. Choose any of these tests:

  • Navigate around the rockfalls (Reason): Eirik warned of two rockfalls. The first is minor (scramble over rubble). The second, near Kross-stead, requires a full detour through an unmarked goat-track.
  • Endure altitude and thinning air (Might): The mountains are high and the air is thin. Headaches, nosebleeds, and shortness of breath. Pace management matters.
  • Cross the Gorge Bridge (Agility): A stone bridge spans a gorge 60 feet deep. The western footing has shifted — the bridge tilts slightly, and stones crumble with each step. Cross quickly, test balance, or find an anchor-line solution.
  • Find shelter from a mountain storm (Intuition): A storm sweeps in without warning. Whiteout conditions, wind strong enough to push characters off the path.
  • Spot and avoid an avalanche zone (Intuition): The slope above is loaded with snow. The vibration of footsteps could trigger a slide.
  • Improvise a bridge across a collapsed road section (Reason): A 10-foot gap over nothing. Use timber, rope, and ingenuity.
complications.round2:
Round 2 — Titan's Stair (Days 9–10)

The party reaches the Stair itself — ice-slick switchbacks carved into the mountain face, narrowing to two abreast, with a sheer drop on one side. Choose any of these tests:

  • Traverse the Titan's Stair switchbacks (Agility): Ice-slick switchbacks, sheer drop, two abreast at best. One slip means a very long fall.
  • Keep the group moving through exhaustion (Presence): Day 9. Everyone is tired, cold, running low on supplies. Moral strength matters as much as physical.
  • Handle the ice-bridge at the summit (Agility or Reason): A natural ice-bridge over the top of the pass. It groans underfoot. Test the ice (Reason) for safest path or trust balance (Agility) and move fast.
  • Descend safely to the Interior Belt (Agility): Steep and loose — scree, ice-melt channels, treacherous footing. A controlled descent requires skill; rushed invites broken ankles.
  • Scout for threats on the descent (Intuition): From the heights, spot Stairwatch below and potentially anyone waiting for the party's arrival.
  • Forage for emergency supplies (Intuition): Slim pickings: mountain hare, snowberry bushes, frozen lichen. Enough to survive, not to thrive.

outcomes.round1:
Interlude: The Watched Feeling

As the party crests a hill, a feeling settles over the group — a prickling at the back of the neck. Not a sound. Not a movement. Just the unmistakable sense that something out there knows where you are. The ridge to the northwest is empty. The sky is grey. Nothing moves. But the feeling doesn't leave.

This is the first effect of Grafvitnir's network. Intelligence from Hrafnborg has reached agents in the field. The PCs are being tracked by corrupted couriers, compromised Chainwarden posts, and Wyrd-touched carrion birds with silver eyes. If anyone uses magic to scan: nothing registers. Grafvitnir's agents are mortal.

Point of Interest — Kross-stead (Day 7–8): An evacuated mining settlement. The shaft-head is open; warm air rises. The walls in the lower shafts are veined with a dark, root-like growth that pulses with amber light — connected to Yggdrasil's root system, though the PCs cannot know this yet. Anyone who touches the growth feels a vibration: like a heartbeat, very slow, very deep. The last logbook entry reads: "The walls of the lower gallery are warm to the touch. Supervisor Alrek says it's geothermal. Alrek is an idiot. Trees don't grow underground."
outcomes.round2:
Interlude: The Cairn Field

Near the summit of Titan's Stair, the path widens into a flat plateau. Dozens of cairns stand here — rough-stacked stone pillars from knee-height to taller than a person. Each marked with runes. These are travel-cairns — monuments left by generations of survivors. The tradition: add a stone and speak a name for safe passage. Some are recent. One is barely a day old — a small stack with a child's mitten tucked between the stones.

The newest cairn has no runes. Just a word scratched into the topstone with a knife: HELP.

If any PC adds a stone to the cairn field, the wind drops for exactly one minute — long enough to feel the sun's warmth through the clouds, faint but real.

Montage 2 Outcomes:

Total Success (5+ before failure limit): 2 Victories. Arrive in Stairwatch with supplies and energy. The party is harder than when they left.

Partial Success (failure limit hit, 2+ more successes than failures): 1 Victory. Choose one cost: Heat (lose 2 days' rations), Weight (one PC's equipment damaged, needs repair), or Word (+1 to future Grafvitnir Pressure Checks — spotted on the descent).

Total Failure: 0 Victories. Lose remaining rations AND one piece of equipment breaks. Grafvitnir's agents know exactly where the party is — the next Pressure Check triggers automatically at 5+. One PC arrives with a condition (exhausted, frostbitten, or rattled). Director gains 3 Malice for the next combat.

Optional Goblin Ambush: Triggers on Total Failure or at Director's discretion on Partial Success. See goblin-monarch, goblin-warrior, goblin-runner stat blocks. Negotiation possible (hard Presence test) — goblins accept half remaining rations in exchange for passage and provide a crude cave-bark map showing something driving creatures out of the mountains toward the southwest.
